title: Chicken Enchiladas

overview.description: Soft tortillas filled with seasoned chicken, covered in a smoky homemade enchilada sauce and baked with cheese until bubbling.

ingredients: Salt and black pepper, to taste
method: Heat the oil over a medium heat.
Whisk in the flour and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the tomato purée and cook for another minute.
Gradually whisk in the chicken stock until smooth.
Add the cumin, paprika, coriander, oregano, garlic powder, onion powder and cayenne (if using).
Simmer for 8–10 minutes until lightly thickened.
Taste and season with salt and black pepper.
Mix a few spoonfuls of the sauce with the shredded chicken.
Fill each tortilla with the chicken and a little cheese, then roll tightly.
Arrange seam-side down in a baking dish.
Bake for 20–25 minutes until bubbling and golden.

learning.did-you-know: Traditional Mexican enchiladas are often dipped in sauce before filling, whereas many Tex-Mex versions are baked covered in sauce and cheese.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to make a quick homemade enchilada sauce and assemble enchiladas that stay moist during baking.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: An excellent way to turn leftover cooked chicken into a comforting family meal using ingredients already found in the Chapman Kitchen.
chapman-tips: Mixing a little sauce into the filling keeps the chicken moist.
Warm the tortillas briefly if they crack when rolling.
Homemade enchilada sauce takes only a few minutes and tastes much fresher than jarred versions.
Leftover fajita chicken works brilliantly in this recipe.
notes.batch-cooking: Assemble, cool and freeze before baking, or bake first and freeze individual portions.
notes.leftovers: Reheat covered in the oven until piping hot.
